text.rule: Compose the eval set from three named slices: GOLD (real user inputs labeled by humans), ADVERSARIAL (handwritten edge cases that broke prod or stress the feature), DRIFT (a rotating slice sampled monthly from live traffic); a set missing any slice is rejected at code review.
Minimum sizes: GOLD ≥50 rows, ADVERSARIAL ≥20 rows, DRIFT ≥30 rows; below these counts the CI gate is advisory only and cannot block merges.

Each row is a 4-tuple: input, expected_output (or rubric), tags (intent / language / hazard class), source (ticket id, prompt, or sampling timestamp); rows without a source field are non-traceable and removed.

Choose ONE judge per row class: heuristic (regex / JSON-schema / exact-match) for structured outputs, LLM-as-judge with a written rubric for open-ended, human review for hazard / safety / brand rows; mixing judge types within one row class is forbidden — calibration is impossible.

Version the eval set in git under `evals/<feature>/vN/`; bumps require a changelog entry listing rows added/removed/relabeled and the reason; in-place edits to old versions are blocked by pre-commit hook.

CI runs the harness on every PR touching feature code or prompts; the gate fails if GOLD pass-rate drops by >2 absolute points vs main OR any ADVERSARIAL row regresses from pass to fail; thresholds are committed to the repo, not stored in CI config.
Re-calibrate the LLM-as-judge against a 30-row human-labeled subsample quarterly; if judge agreement with human falls below 80%, freeze automated gating until rubric or judge model is revised.
text.example: Team ships a customer-support copilot. eval/copilot/v3/ contains: gold.jsonl (78 real tickets with expected intent + ideal response), adversarial.jsonl (24 jailbreak attempts + PII leakage probes + multi-turn confusion), drift.jsonl (32 rows sampled from last month). Judges: regex on intent slot, LLM-as-judge on response quality with 6-criterion rubric, human review on the PII leakage rows. CI gate: GOLD intent ≥92%, GOLD response ≥3.6/5, ADVERSARIAL pass-rate cannot drop. A PR changing the system prompt drops adversarial from 24/24 to 22/24 — CI blocks merge.
